dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8249 IH: Making Reviews Certain Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Gottheimer introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 to clarify the scope of review, establish limits for judicial review of environmental documents relating to energy infrastructure, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Making Reviews Certain Act.
Section 106 of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. 4336) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by inserting ; scope of review after level of review; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: In preparing an environmental document for a proposed agency action, a Federal agency is only required to consider those effects that share a reasonably close causal relationship to, and are proximately caused by, the immediate project or action under consideration, to comply with the requirements of this Act.

section.header: Limitations on judicial review of environmental documents relating to energy infrastructure
section.text: Title I of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. 4331 et seq.) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating section 112 as section 110A and moving such section so as to appear after section 110; and
by inserting before section 111 the following:

section.header: Limitations on judicial review of environmental documents relating to energy infrastructure

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law (except as provided in subparagraph (A) with respect to a shorter deadline), a claim challenging whether a final agency action relating to energy infrastructure complies with the requirements of this Act shall be barred unless—

subparagraph.text: such claim is filed not later than 180 days after the final agency action is made public, unless a shorter deadline is specified under law;
in the case of a final agency action for which there was a public comment period on an environmental document, such claim—

clause.text: is filed by a party that submitted a substantive and unique comment during such public comment period by the noticed comment deadline for the environmental document and such comment was sufficiently detailed to put the applicable Federal agency on notice of the issue upon which the party seeks review; and
concerns the same subject matter raised in the comment submitted during the public comment period;

subparagraph.text: such claim is filed by a party that has suffered or imminently will suffer direct harm from the final agency action; and
such claim does not challenge the establishment of a categorical exclusion.

paragraph.text: In this subsection, the term energy infrastructure means a facility, and associated equipment, used for enabling the identification, leasing, development, production, processing, transportation, transmission, refining, and generation needed for energy.
Notwithstanding any other provision of law, no final agency action shall be vacated by a court, following a decision on the merits, unless the court determines that—

subparagraph.text: the major Federal action for which the environmental document or final agency action is prepared will pose a significant risk of a proximate and substantial environmental harm; and
there is no other equitable remedy available as a matter of law.

paragraph.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, if a court determines there are errors or deficiencies with an environmental document or final agency action that need to be corrected—

subparagraph.text: the court may remand the environmental document to the applicable Federal agency with specific instruction to correct such errors or deficiencies within 180 days from the date on which the order of the court was issued to the applicable Federal agency; and

the major Federal action may be carried out pursuant to the final agency action notwithstanding the remand of the environmental document or final agency action under subparagraph (A), including during the time prescribed by the court to the Federal agency to correct such errors or deficiencies, so long as the court has not determined that vacatur is appropriate under paragraph (1).

subsection.header: No effect on review of compliance with other deadlines
subsection.text: This section shall not affect the right to obtain review under section 107(g)(3).

section.header: Deference to agencies in judicial review
section.text: Section 111 of the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (42 U.S.C. 4336e) is amended by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: To comply with the requirements of this Act, the term reasonably foreseeable, with respect to environmental effects of a proposed agency action, means effects that share a reasonably close causal relationship to, and are proximately caused by, the immediate project or action under consideration.
